input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Tractive System`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Tractive System` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.6.1.2
            TITLE: contact with any tractive system connections with a 100 mm long, 6 mm diameter insulated

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.1.2: Contact with any Tractive System connections with a 100 mm long, 6 mm diameter insulated
EV.6.1.2 Contact with any Tractive System connections with a 100 mm long, 6 mm diameter insulated 
test probe must not be possible when the Tractive System enclosures are in place.

RULE: ev.7.8.2
            TITLE: additional interlocks may be included in the tractive system or components

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.8.2: Additional Interlocks may be included in the Tractive System or components
EV.7.8.2 Additional Interlocks may be included in the Tractive System or components

RULE: ev.5.2.1
            TITLE: all tractive system components must be rated for the maximum tractive system voltage

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.2.1: All Tractive System components must be rated for the maximum Tractive System voltage
EV.5.2.1 All Tractive System components must be rated for the maximum Tractive System voltage

RULE: ev.4.8
            TITLE: tractive system part positioning

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.8: Tractive System Part Positioning
EV.4.8 Tractive System Part Positioning 
All parts belonging to the Tractive System must meet F.10.5.8

RULE: ev.6.5.1
            TITLE: separation of tractive system and glv system:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.5.1: Separation of Tractive System and GLV System:
EV.6.5.1 Separation of Tractive System and GLV System: 
a. The entire Tractive System and GLV System must be completely galvanically separated. 
b. The border between Tractive and GLV System is the galvanic isolation between both 
systems.  Therefore, some components, such as the Motor Controller, may be part of 
both systems.

RULE: ev.9.3.2
            TITLE: tractive system active must not be possible until both:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.9.3.2: Tractive System Active must not be possible until both:
EV.9.3.2 Tractive System Active must not be possible until both: 
• GLV System is Energized 
• Shutdown Circuit is Closed
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 107 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: in.4.4.1
            TITLE: the insulation monitoring device will be tested by connecting a resistor between the tractive

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.4.4.1: The Insulation Monitoring Device will be tested by connecting a resistor between the Tractive
IN.4.4.1 The Insulation Monitoring Device will be tested by connecting a resistor between the Tractive 
System Measuring Points (EV.5.8), and several electrically conductive vehicle parts while the 
Tractive System is active

RULE: t.1.9.2
            TITLE: tractive system firewalls must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.9.2: Tractive System Firewalls must be:
T.1.9.2 Tractive System Firewalls must be:  
a. Made of aluminum 
b. Grounded, refer to EV.6.7 Grounding

RULE: ev.5.6.3
            TITLE: the tractive system must contain a discharge circuit. the discharge circuit must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.6.3: The Tractive System must contain a Discharge Circuit. The Discharge Circuit must be:
EV.5.6.3 The Tractive System must contain a Discharge Circuit. The Discharge Circuit must be: 
a. Wired in a way that it is always active when the Shutdown Circuit is open 
b. Able to discharge the Intermediate Circuit capacitors if the HVD has been opened  
c. Not be fused 
d. Designed to handle the maximum Tractive System voltage for minimum 15 seconds

RULE: ev.6.4.1
            TITLE: all tractive system connections must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.4.1: All Tractive System connections must:
EV.6.4.1 All Tractive System connections must: 
a. Be designed to use intentional current paths through conductors designed for electrical 
current  
b. Not rely on steel bolts to be the primary conductor 
c. Not include compressible material such as plastic in the stack-up

RULE: ev.1.1
            TITLE: tractive system – ts

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.1.1: Tractive System – TS
EV.1.1 Tractive System – TS  
Every part electrically connected to the Motor(s) and/or Accumulator(s)

RULE: ev.3.2.3
            TITLE: all tractive system power must flow through the energy meter

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.3.2.3: All Tractive System power must flow through the Energy Meter
EV.3.2.3 All Tractive System power must flow through the Energy Meter

RULE: in.12.3.3
            TITLE: the tractive system active light may switch a short time after the vehicle has come to a

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.12.3.3: The Tractive System Active Light may switch a short time after the vehicle has come to a
IN.12.3.3 The Tractive System Active Light may switch a short time after the vehicle has come to a 
complete stop as the reduction of the system voltage is not immediate.  See EV.7.2.2.c

RULE: ev.5.9.1
            TITLE: the vehicle must include a tractive systems active light (tsal) that must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.9.1: The vehicle must include a Tractive Systems Active Light (TSAL) that must:
EV.5.9.1 The vehicle must include a Tractive Systems Active Light (TSAL) that must: 
a. Illuminate when the GLV System is energized to indicate the status of the Tractive 
System 
b. Be directly controlled by the voltage present in the Tractive System using hard wired 
electronics.  Software control is not permitted. 
c. Not perform any other functions.

RULE: ev.7.2.3
            TITLE: when the ams, imd or bspd open the shutdown circuit:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.2.3: When the AMS, IMD or BSPD Open the Shutdown Circuit:
EV.7.2.3 When the AMS, IMD or BSPD Open the Shutdown Circuit: 
a. The Tractive System must stay disabled until manually reset  
b. The Tractive System must be reset only by manual action of a person directly at the 
vehicle  
c. The driver must not be able to reactivate the Tractive System from inside the vehicle  
d. Operation of the Shutdown Buttons or TSMS must not reset the Shutdown Circuit

RULE: ev.7.6.4
            TITLE: the imd must monitor the tractive system for:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.6.4: The IMD must monitor the Tractive System for:
EV.7.6.4 The IMD must monitor the Tractive System for: 
a. An isolation failure
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 104 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
b. A failure in the IMD operation 
This must be done without the influence of any programmable logic.

RULE: ev.4.9.2
            TITLE: if the material of the housing containing parts of the tractive system is electrically conductive,

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.9.2: If the material of the housing containing parts of the Tractive System is electrically conductive,
EV.4.9.2 If the material of the housing containing parts of the Tractive System is electrically conductive, 
it must have a low resistance connection to GLV System Ground, see EV.6.7

RULE: ev.4.9.1
            TITLE: every housing or enclosure containing parts of the tractive system other than motor

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.9.1: Every housing or enclosure containing parts of the Tractive System other than Motor
EV.4.9.1 Every housing or enclosure containing parts of the Tractive System other than Motor 
housings, must be labelled with the:   
a. Symbol specified in ISO 7010-W012  (triangle with black lightning bolt on yellow 
background)  
b. Text “High Voltage” if the voltage meets T.9.1.1

RULE: in.4.5.1
            TITLE: the insulation resistance between the tractive system and glv system ground will be

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.4.5.1: The insulation resistance between the Tractive System and GLV System Ground will be
IN.4.5.1 The insulation resistance between the Tractive System and GLV System Ground will be 
measured.

RULE: t.1.9.1
            TITLE: tractive system firewalls must meet the requirements of t.1.8 above

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.9.1: Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above
T.1.9.1 Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above
